title: Conversion Error of a DATE column when reading a file to insert in table
description:
### What happens?

Error similar to issue #13660.

I created an empty table in DuckDB and tried to upload a CSV to it, which contains the valid ISO 8601 date (2022-03-05). It threw the following exception:

```python
ConversionException                       Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[9], line 2
      1 source = "data/bug report"
----> 2 duckdb.sql(f"""
      3            COPY df_input
      4            FROM "{source}"
      5            (
      6                AUTO_DETECT FALSE,
      7                DATE_FORMAT "YYYY-MM-DD",
      8                DELIMITER '|',
      9                HEADER FALSE
     10             );
     11            """)
 
ConversionException: Conversion Error: CSV Error on Line: 1
Original Line: XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-03-25|48396|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|debit|||||||||||||||||||||
Error when converting column "date_tx". Could not convert string "2022-03-25" to 'DATE'

Column date_tx is being converted as type DATE
This type was auto-detected from the CSV file.
Possible solutions:
* Override the type for this column manually by setting the type explicitly, e.g. types={'date_tx': 'VARCHAR'}
* Set the sample size to a larger value to enable the auto-detection to scan more values, e.g. sample_size=-1
* Use a COPY statement to automatically derive types from an existing table.
* Check whether the null string value is set correctly (e.g., nullstr = 'N/A')

  file = data/bug report
  delimiter = | (Set By User)
  quote = " (Auto-Detected)
  escape = \0 (Auto-Detected)
  new_line = \r\n (Auto-Detected)
  header = false (Set By User)
  skip_rows = 0 (Auto-Detected)
  comment = \0 (Auto-Detected)
  strict_mode = true (Auto-Detected)
  date_format = YYYY-MM-DD (Set By User)
  timestamp_format =  (Auto-Detected)
  null_padding = 0
  sample_size = 20480
  ignore_errors = false
  all_varchar = 0
```

### To Reproduce

### File

`my.csv`:

```
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-03-25|48396|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|debit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-02-12|36486|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-08-20|17848|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-09-11|41051|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-07-16|29621|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|debit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-10-21|25258|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-04-14|170137|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|debit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-11-09|14817|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-06-06|88758|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
XX|1|123456789|1111111|USD|BILLETERA|3000000000|2022-03-05|99663|RETIRO CORRESPONSAL|credit|||||||||||||||||||||
```

### Code:


```sql
CREATE TABLE df_input (
  country CHAR(2),
  Idtype INT NOT NULL,
  Idnumber INT NOT NULL,
  transaction_id INT NOT NULL,
  currency_type CHAR(3),
  product_type VARCHAR(50),
  accountNumber CHAR(10) NOT NULL,
  date_tx DATE NOT NULL,
  amount FLOAT NOT NULL,
  description VARCHAR(254) NOT NULL,
  transaction_class VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL,
  card_number INT,
  card_type VARCHAR(7),
  initial_card_quote BOOLEAN,
  issuing_bank INT,
  franchise VARCHAR(16),
  private_brand VARCHAR(100),
  card_product VARCHAR(12),
  transaction_type VARCHAR(50),
  business_name VARCHAR(150),
  transaction_state VARCHAR(10),
  denial_grounds VARCHAR(100),
  purchasing_channel VARCHAR(50),
  purchasing_country VARCHAR(15),
  number_installments INT,
  initial_balance DOUBLE,
  final_balance DOUBLE NOT NULL,
  authorization_copy INT,
  authorization_date TIMESTAMP,
  authorization_channel VARCHAR(50),
  authorization_type VARCHAR(8)
  );
copy df_input
from 'my.csv' (
                       AUTO_DETECT FALSE,
                       DATE_FORMAT "YYYY-MM-DD",
                       DELIMITER '|',
                       HEADER FALSE
                    );
```

### OS:

Windows 10 22H2 (10.0.19045) x64

### DuckDB Version:

v1.2.1

### DuckDB Client:

Python

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Marcio Padilla

### Affiliation:

Experian Colombia

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE df_input (country CHAR(2), Idtype INT NOT NULL, Idnumber INT NOT NULL, transaction_id INT NOT NULL, currency_type CHAR(3), product_type VARCHAR(50), accountNumber CHAR(10) NOT NULL, date_tx DATE NOT NULL, amount FLOAT NOT NULL, description VARCHAR(254) NOT NULL, transaction_class VARCHAR(8) NOT NULL, card_number INT, card_type VARCHAR(7), initial_card_quote BOOLEAN, issuing_bank INT, franchise VARCHAR(16), private_brand VARCHAR(100), card_product VARCHAR(12), transaction_type VARCHAR(50), business_name VARCHAR(150), transaction_state VARCHAR(10), denial_grounds VARCHAR(100), purchasing_channel VARCHAR(50), purchasing_country VARCHAR(15), number_installments INT, initial_balance DOUBLE PRECISION, final_balance DOUBLE NOT NULL, authorization_copy INT, authorization_date TIMESTAMP, authorization_channel VARCHAR(50), authorization_type VARCHAR(8));
COPY df_input FROM 'my.csv' DELIMITER '|' CSV HEADER;
SELECT * FROM df_input WHERE date_tx IS NULL;

INSERT INTO df_input (country, Idtype, Idnumber, transaction_id, currency_type, product_type, accountNumber, date_tx, amount, description) VALUES ('XX', 1, 123456789, 1111111, 'USD', 'BILLETERA', '3000000000', '2022-03-05', 3000.00, 'Test transaction');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM df_input WHERE date_tx > '2022-01-01' AND date_tx < '2022-12-31';
key_bug_features: Conversion Error of DATE column from CSV file
